staticProofs.claim: Current Next.js headers apply the configured Access-Control-Allow-Origin value globally.
OAuth consent is a noindex website page used for browser authorization review.
The OAuth decision endpoint is a POST-only same-origin API that forwards the consent decision to Supabase.
Pixelfed first-login docs route the browser through the consent page and same-origin decision API.
Mochi Pets uses an iframe plus strict postMessage origin checks, not permissive CORS.

recommendation.smallestFuturePattern: /api/:path* is the narrowest candidate if future browser evidence proves any cross-origin JSON route needs CORS; current OAuth, social handoff, and Mochi Pets evidence does not prove public pages or static assets need it.
recommendation.keepUntilBrowserProof: Do not narrow the global header until OAuth consent approve/deny, Pixelfed OIDC return, and Mochi Pets iframe/postMessage are browser-tested on a Vercel preview.
